description_long: This vase features a tall, slender neck that flares dramatically into a wide, trumpet-shaped mouth, giving it an elegant and classical silhouette. The body is bulbous and tapers to a pointed base, suggesting it may be inspired by ancient amphorae or ceremonial vessels. Its surface is decorated with a weathered, mottled pattern of terracotta red and faded blue bands, with significant areas of surface wear and patina, indicating age or intentional antiquing. The mouth and neck are predominantly red, with a blue band encircling the upper neck and a broader blue band around the midsection of the body. The finish is matte and rough, with visible textural irregularities and patches of discoloration. This vase would be a striking decorative object in an urban park, plaza, or public garden, evoking a sense of history and artistry.
description: A tall, slender-necked vase with a flared mouth, decorated in weathered red and blue bands with a rough, aged surface. The body is bulbous and tapers to a pointed base, giving it a classical, antique appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height, flared mouth, and the prominent blue and red banding with heavy surface wear.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated, this view highlights the curvature of the body and the continuity of the blue band around the midsection.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the symmetry of the vase and the even distribution of the weathered texture across the surface.
description_view_3: Similar to the front, this view shows the opposite side with consistent banding and patina, revealing no significant asymmetry.
attribute_pattern: horizontal color bands with mottled wear
